14.reason: [The increment of 'n' four times in one scanf leads to incorrect indexing and skips array elements; should increment 'n' only once per segment]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The increment of 'n' four times in one scanf leads to incorrect indexing and skips array elements; should increment 'n' only once per segment]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The increment of 'n' four times in one scanf causes incorrect indexing; it should be incremented once per segment.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The use of n++ four times in one statement leads to undefined behavior due to multiple unsequenced modifications to n. Also, input order is wrong: should read x1,y1,x2,y2 but reads x1,x2,y1,y2.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The use of n++ four times in one statement causes undefined behavior and incorrect indexing; it reads input into wrong array elements.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The increment of 'n' four times in one scanf leads to incorrect indexing and skips array elements. It should be incremented only once per segment.]
